actor_notes: Full board overseeing strategic alternatives, committee formation, and merger approval.
first_evidence_text: Upon receiving a report of this meeting, in recognition of its public company obligations, the Board appointed a subcommittee of two independent directors, Frank W. Barrett and Richard W. Anderson, to interview potential investment bankers to represent the Company in possible negotiations with Party A and to assist the Board in evaluating additional strategic options for the realization of shareholder value, including potential change in control transactions.

actor_notes: Two-independent-director subcommittee: Frank W. Barrett and Richard W. Anderson.
first_evidence_text: Upon receiving a report of this meeting, in recognition of its public company obligations, the Board appointed a subcommittee of two independent directors, Frank W. Barrett and Richard W. Anderson, to interview potential investment bankers to represent the Company in possible negotiations with Party A and to assist the Board in evaluating additional strategic options for the realization of shareholder value, including potential change in control transactions.

actor_notes: Independent-director committee chaired by Frank W. Barrett; members Richard W. Anderson and James C. Garvey.
first_evidence_text: In light of the potentially significant time and effort involved in pursuing such exploratory transaction strategy, as well as the possible need to provide guidance to GHF and management on short notice, at the conclusion of the meeting, the Board appointed a special committee of independent directors of the Board (which we refer to as the “Transaction Committee”), composed of Frank W. Barrett (Chair), Richard W. Anderson and James C. Garvey, for the purpose of assisting in the supervision and oversight of the process on the full Board’s behalf and making a recommendation to the full Board.

actor_notes: Company financial advisor; Greene Holcomb & Fisher LLC was acquired by BMO on August 1, 2016.
first_evidence_text: The subcommittee interviewed several investment banking firms, including Greene Holcomb & Fisher LLC (the business of which was subsequently acquired by BMO Capital Markets Corp. and which we refer to as “GHF” prior to such acquisition and “BMO” thereafter).

actor_notes: Company legal counsel.
first_evidence_text: Members of management and representatives of each of GHF and the Company’s legal counsel, Hinckley, Allen & Snyder LLP (which we refer to as “Hinckley Allen”), participated in all meetings of the Transaction Committee, except as otherwise indicated.

actor_notes: G&W legal counsel during merger negotiations.
first_evidence_text: On August 10, 2016, representatives of Hinckley Allen and Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP (which we refer to as “Simpson Thacher”), G&W’s legal counsel, discussed various provisions of the draft merger agreement and voting agreement that had been provided to G&W on August 3, 2016, and Hinckley Allen provided Simpson Thacher with a revised disclosure letter to accompany the August 3 draft merger agreement.

actor_notes: Class I rail partner that initiated early strategic discussions and was included in the initial outreach cohort.
first_evidence_text: met with one of the Company’s Class I rail partners (“Party A”) to discuss joint commercial opportunities. During the meeting, Party A suggested possible joint venture arrangements and expressed some interest in acquiring equity in the Company to further develop strategic opportunities and enhance regional connectivity.

actor_notes: Strategic bidder and eventual merger counterparty.
first_evidence_text: Between April 3, 2016 and April 6, 2016, representatives of GHF and members of the Company’s management held a series of introductory meetings with five potential strategic buyers (including G&W) at the American Short Line and Regional Railroad Association’s 2016 Connections Convention (“2016 Connections Convention”).

actor_notes: Late-stage strategic bidder that declined to raise price after G&W’s revised offer.
first_evidence_text: Subsequently, on April 21, 2016, the Company and representatives of GHF held an introductory meeting with another potential strategic buyer (“Party B”).

actor_notes: Late-entering strategic bidder that approached management in early July 2016.
first_evidence_text: In early July 2016, a potential strategic buyer that had not previously been part of the process (“Party C”) approached the Company’s management and expressed interest in acquiring the Company. After executing a confidentiality agreement, Party C was provided the memorandum concerning the Company provided to the other potential buyers.

actor_notes: Financial buyer that reengaged with a revised LOI but stopped when the Company would not grant exclusivity.
first_evidence_text: Party D (a financial buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $21.00 per share (subject to a four-week diligence period).

actor_notes: Strategic buyer that submitted an LOI and later a revised LOI with financing support from Party F.
first_evidence_text: Party E (a strategic buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $21.26 per share (subject to a 60-day exclusivity period for due diligence and negotiation of definitive documentation).

actor_notes: Strategic buyer that later provided financing support for Party E’s revised LOI.
first_evidence_text: Party F (a strategic buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $19.20 per share (subject to a 30-day due diligence period).

actor_notes: Placeholder for one of the remaining unidentified strategic buyers from the 11 strategic buyers that executed confidentiality agreements after Party A, G&W, Party B, Party E, and Party F are separately tracked; late entrant Party C is tracked separately outside that initial cohort.
first_evidence_text: During the week of March 28, 2016, in accordance with the Transaction Committee’s directives, representatives of GHF contacted 11 potential strategic buyers (including Party A) and 18 potential financial buyers. Each of the potential strategic buyers and 14 potential financial buyers subsequently executed confidentiality agreements.

actor_notes: Placeholder for one of the remaining unidentified strategic buyers from the 11 strategic buyers that executed confidentiality agreements after Party A, G&W, Party B, Party E, and Party F are separately tracked; late entrant Party C is tracked separately outside that initial cohort.
